payload.name: plant
payload.description: Grow capabilities from a spec (the browser's /plant page, or argv). argv[1] is a seed's events as JSONL (one {name,payload} per line), a JSON array, or a single object. Emit each well-formed event as-is so the kernel's strange loop compiles any command.declared / projector.declared into real capabilities and replays the rest. Then emit a seed.planted receipt summarizing the planting (seed='ui', the command/projector names, events_replayed) — the same shape `self ls seeds` reads, so a UI planting shows up there too. On a parse error or an empty/invalid spec, emit a seed.planted with ok=false and an error message instead, so the page can show what went wrong. Compiling the declarations needs a brain wired on /setup; the plant itself only emits.

params.spec: string — declarations as JSONL / JSON array / single object

examples.note: a single command.declared is emitted as-is plus a seed.planted receipt
unparseable spec → a seed.planted error, nothing emitted
valid JSON that isn't an event → no events found

payload.name: plant
payload.description: The grow-a-capability page. Render, with zero JavaScript: (1) a spec <textarea name='spec'> prefilled with a working trio, in a form POSTing to /run/plant; (2) one-click 'starter' forms that carry a ready-made trio in a hidden spec field; (3) the capabilities already in the garden, read from command.declared / projector.declared (skip the kernel's own pages); (4) a log of recent plantings from seed.planted (ok / failed + what was planted). Bare semantic HTML + the kernel class vocabulary (card, muted, tag, row, danger); the kernel injects the theme at serve.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # plant (projector) — the grow-a-capability page. It reads the event stream and # renders, with zero JavaScript: # - a spec box prefilled with a working trio → POSTs to /run/plant # - one-click "starter" buttons that plant a ready-made minimal capability # - the capabilities already in the garden (from the *.declared events) # - a log of recent plantings (seed.planted) # Bare semantic HTML + the kernel's class vocabulary; the kernel injects the theme. import sys, json from html import escape # Pages the kernel owns — don't list them as "your capabilities". SYSTEM = {"plant", "setup", "interview", "welcome", "kernel", "configure", "answer"} def trio(cmd, event, proj, consumes, cmd_desc, proj_desc, params, fields): """A full trio (command.declared + projector.declared) as JSONL — the unit a seed plants. Built as Python dicts and dumped, so there's no hand-escaped JSON.""" return "\n".join(json.dumps(x, ensure_ascii=False) for x in [ {"name": "command.declared", "payload": { "name": cmd, "description": cmd_desc, "params": params, "event": {"name": event, "fields": fields}}}, {"name": "projector.declared", "payload": { "name": proj, "description": proj_desc, "consumes": [event]}}, ]) # The prefilled template: a note-taker. Rename things and it's yours. TEMPLATE = trio( "note", "note.added", "notes", ["note.added"], "Append a note. Join argv into the text; emit note.added {text}.", "List every note.added as a simple feed, with a form posting to /run/note.", {"text": "string — the note, joined from argv"}, {"text": "string"}) # One-click starters — pick instead of write. Each plants a minimal trio. STARTERS = [ ("counter", trio( "tick", "counter.ticked", "counter", ["counter.ticked"], "Increment a named counter. argv[1] is the counter name; emit counter.ticked {name}.", "Tally counter.ticked by name into a table, with a form to add a tick.", {"name": "string — which counter"}, {"name": "string"})), ("guestbook", trio( "sign", "guestbook.signed", "guestbook", ["guestbook.signed"], "Sign the guestbook. argv: ; emit guestbook.signed {who, message}.", "Render guestbook.signed entries as a message wall, newest last, with a sign form.", {"who": "string", "message": "string"}, {"who": "string", "message": "string"})), ] commands, projectors, plantings = {}, {}, [] for line in sys.stdin: line = line.strip() if not line: continue try: e = json.loads(line) except json.JSONDecodeError: continue n = e.get("name") p = e.get("payload", {}) or {} if n == "command.declared": commands[p.get("name", "?")] = p.get("description", "") elif n == "projector.declared": projectors[p.get("name", "?")] = p.get("description", "") elif n == "seed.planted": plantings.append(p) print("plant") print("

plant a capability

") print("

Grow self from the browser. Paste a spec — a seed's " "command.declared / projector.declared events, as " "JSONL or JSON — and plant it. The kernel's strange loop compiles the " "declarations into real, running capabilities (a brain must be wired on " "/setup for the compile). The loop carries the spec; the compiler writes the code.

") # 1) Write a spec. print("

write a spec

") print("
") print("") print("" % escape(TEMPLATE)) print("") print("
") # 2) Pick a starter (zero-JS: one form per starter, spec carried in a hidden field). print("

or pick a starter

") print("

One click plants a ready-made minimal capability you can use right away.

") print("
") for label, spec in STARTERS: print("
") print("" % escape(spec)) print("" % escape(label)) print("
") print("
") # 3) What's already growing here. print("

in your garden

") grown = [(n, d) for n, d in sorted(commands.items()) if n not in SYSTEM] grown_p = [(n, d) for n, d in sorted(projectors.items()) if n not in SYSTEM] if not grown and not grown_p: print("

No grown capabilities yet — plant one above.

") else: print("") for n, d in grown: print("" % (escape(n), escape((d or "").split(".")[0]))) for n, d in grown_p: print("" % (escape(n), escape(n), escape((d or "").split(".")[0]))) print("
kindnamewhat it does
command%s%s
projector%s%s
") # 4) Recent plantings (newest last, mirroring the log). if plantings: print("

recent plantings

") print("") for p in plantings[-8:]: ok = p.get("ok", True) mark = "ok" if ok else "failed" what = [] if p.get("commands"): what.append("commands: " + ", ".join(p["commands"])) if p.get("projectors"): what.append("projections: " + ", ".join(p["projectors"])) if p.get("error"): what.append(escape(p["error"])) if not what: what.append("(nothing)") cls = "tag" if ok else "tag danger" print("" % (cls, mark, "; ".join(what))) print("
planted
%s%s
") print("")

examples.note: renders the spec form, the prefilled template, starters, and the garden
